description: A vertical designed graphic card combining a photo background, a slogan headline, and a quoted news excerpt, in DOL's branded style. The upper portion is a desaturated/blue-tinted photo of a person (seen from behind/side) wearing a welding helmet flipped up, working in an industrial shop. Across the lower-middle is a headline in white serif type 'Skilled Trades SURGE!' ('Skilled Trades' in italic, 'SURGE!' bold). Below sits a white rounded text box containing the blue 'KARE 11' (Minneapolis NBC affiliate) news logo and the quoted/paraphrased article text in black serif: 'A new school year has officially started and with it, new numbers that show a surge in trade schools. New data from Validated Insights, a higher education marketing firm, reveals a significant increase in trade school enrollment.' A faint white DOL seal appears centered at the bottom. This is a designed messaging card using borrowed local-news credibility to promote the administration's trade-school/apprenticeship narrative. Messaging technique: third-party news citation plus an upbeat slogan. Target audience: young people, parents, and workers considering the trades.
notable_text: Skilled Trades SURGE!; KARE 11; A new school year has officially started... New data from Validated Insights... reveals a significant increase in trade school enrollment.
